---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "tar_extract"
namespace: "Compression"
description: "Extract files from a tar archive"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `tar_extract`  
Namespace: `compression`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/compression/tar_extract)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/compression/tar_extract)

:::




## Argument group

### Arguments

|Name                 |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                              |Attributes                                   |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`            |Input file                                                                                                                                                                                                               |`file`, required, example: `"input.tar.gz"`  |
|`--output`           |Folder to restore file(s) to.                                                                                                                                                                                            |`file`, required, example: `"output_folder"` |
|`--strip_components` |Strip this amount of leading components from file names on extraction. For example, to extract only 'myfile.txt' from an archive containing the structure `this/goes/deep/myfile.txt', use 3 to strip 'this/goes/deep/'. |`integer`, example: `1`                      |
|`--exclude`          |Prevents any file or member whose name matches the shell wildcard (pattern) from being extracted.                                                                                                                        |`string`, example: `"docs/figures"`          |

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/compression/tar_extract/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/compression/tar_extract).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/compression/tar_extract/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
